summary: Verified the new react-three-fiber 3D additions to the LinkOps landing page (Hero TrendSpline3D, Framework + FinalCTA NetworkNodes3D backgrounds). All three 3D canvases mount with correct data-testids and non-zero dimensions, render visually as expected, and produce zero page errors / zero console errors. The previously verified flows (hero CTA -> contact modal -> form fill -> POST /api/leads, header CTA, backend GET /api/ + POST /api/leads) continue to work.
critical_code_review_comments: Lazy-loading the same NetworkNodes3D module separately from Framework.jsx and FinalCTA.jsx will produce two webpack lazy chunks for the same component file. Minor optimisation: import once and reuse the lazy ref, or rely on React.lazy de-duping the dynamic import (it does, but two separate `lazy(() => import(...))` calls each create their own LazyComponent wrapper). Not a bug, just code quality.
Three.js logs `THREE.Clock has been deprecated. Please use THREE.Timer instead.` -- consider pinning compatible versions or migrating; harmless today.

useInView hook does NOT unobserve once the element first enters view. It keeps re-toggling `inView` based on intersection. This means scrolling away unmounts the heavy NetworkNodes3D Canvas (good for perf), but on every re-entry it rebuilds the entire scene -- consider unobserving after first true if scenes are ever stateful. Currently fine since they are stateless background visuals.

TrendSpline3D and NetworkNodes3D both create THREE objects in useMemo and add them to scene in useEffect; this works but could be combined into the useEffect to avoid memo retaining GPU resources if re-renders ever happen; cleanup paths look correct.
seed_data_creation: Created two TEST_-prefixed lead docs via POST /api/leads during validation (TEST_3D_Verify, TEST_3D_Frontend). No cleanup endpoint available; harmless test data only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: 3D scenes use react-three-fiber with imperative THREE objects (no r3f JSX intrinsics inside Canvas) to bypass @emergentbase/visual-edits attribute injection. Test selectors: [data-testid="hero-3d-spline"] (Hero), [data-testid="network-nodes-3d"] (Framework + FinalCTA, lazy-loaded behind IntersectionObserver useInView with rootMargin 400px). Verified in Chromium @ 1920x1080. Backend POST /api/leads returns 200 with full echo + UUID id + ISO created_at.
rca of the issue: N/A -- no issue found. Original concern was that babel plugin @emergentbase/visual-edits injects `x-line-number` attributes on all JSX, which break r3f intrinsics like <mesh>/<group>. The fix already applied (only render <Canvas><SceneController/></Canvas> and build everything imperatively via useThree+THREE.*) is verified working: 3 canvases mount, animate, no React/three errors in console or page errors.
